description: This scenario tracks the public and market sentiment shift from the incumbent Blockbuster to the disruptive newcomer Netflix during 2004-2005. As Netflix's DVD-by-mail model gained traction and Blockbuster struggled with its late-fee-heavy revenue model, consumers transitioned from skeptical about digital/mail-order convenience to favoring the new subscription paradigm.

key_events.description: Netflix reaches 1 million subscribers, signaling critical mass.
Blockbuster announces the removal of late fees in a desperate attempt to stem user churn.
Netflix stock hits a new high while Blockbuster's store count begins its first major decline.
Wall Street analysts officially downgrade Blockbuster in favor of Netflix's growth model.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling how high switching costs and established brand loyalty (incumbent) erode under the pressure of a more efficient, customer-centric business model.
